provenance.title: Pay Attention to MLPs
provenance.abstract_truncated: We propose gMLP, a simple MLP-based architecture with gating (Spatial Gating Unit) that can perform as well as Transformers in key language and vision applications without self-attention. gMLP achieves parity with Transformers on BERT pretraining perplexity and matches DeiT on ImageNet, demonstrating self-attention is not essential for scaling.
key_claims: gMLP (MLPs with gating) matches Transformer performance on BERT pretraining perplexity and ViT image classification.
Self-attention is not a necessary ingredient for scaling up ML models — model capacity matters more.
gMLP scales with data and compute at a rate comparable to Transformers (empirical scaling laws hold for both).
Adding a tiny single-head self-attention (aMLP) to gMLP yields a hybrid that outperforms Transformers of similar capacity on all tested NLP tasks.
gMLPs learn Toeplitz-like (shift-invariant) spatial weight matrices on MLM tasks and locally-receptive spatial weights on vision tasks — both emergent from training, not imposed.

variants.name: gMLP (base)
variants.description: Stack of L identical gMLP blocks. No positional encodings. No self-attention.

variants.name: aMLP
variants.description: gMLP augmented with a single tiny self-attention head (d_attn=64 or 128) injected into the SGU gating branch.

modules.name: gMLP_Block
modules.description: Core repeating unit. Input → LayerNorm → channel proj (expand) → GeLU → SGU → channel proj (contract) → residual add.
pseudocode: x = Linear(x, d_ffn, axis=channel)   # U projection: [B,n,d_model] -> [B,n,d_ffn]
x = Linear(x, d_model, axis=channel)  # V projection back to d_model

modules.name: SGU
modules.description: Splits the hidden dim in two halves. One half (v) undergoes spatial projection (cross-token mixing via W ∈ R^{n×n}), the other half (u) is the multiplicative gate. Output is element-wise product u * (W*v + b).
pseudocode: u, v = split(x, axis=channel)          # each [B, n, d_ffn/2]
v = Linear(v, n, axis=spatial,          # W: [n,n], bias init=1, weight init≈0

key_details.W_init: near-zero (paper uses small random values so W≈0 initially)
key_details.b_init: ones (so fW,b(Z) ≈ 1 initially → SGU ≈ identity at start)
key_details.flop_count: n^2 * e/2 multiply-adds where e = d_ffn

modules.name: Tiny_Attn
modules.description: Used only in aMLP variant. Single-head QKV attention with very small hidden size (64 or 128). Output is projected back to d_out = d_ffn/2 (the same shape as SGU output).
pseudocode: w = einsum('bnd,bmd->bnm', q, k)          # [B,n,n] attention logits
return Linear(x, d_out, axis=channel)     # d_out = d_ffn/2

modules.name: aMLP_SGU
modules.description: Modified SGU for aMLP: the gating branch v is replaced by sum of spatial projection (W*v) AND tiny attention output, then element-wise multiplied with u.
modules.note: Fig 6 shows tiny attention added to the gating side. Exact summation vs concatenation not fully specified; from pseudocode it appears as: gate = spatial_proj(v) + tiny_attn(x_normalized); return u * gate

NLP.padding_mask: not needed — model ignores pads naturally

toeplitz_constraint.description: W is constrained to be a Toeplitz matrix (shift-invariant 1D convolution with full-sequence kernel) because gMLP learns Toeplitz-like W organically on MLM tasks. The constraint is quality-neutral but reduces redundant parameters.

equations.name: gMLP_block_forward
equations.description: Full block computation. X ∈ R^{n×d} is input. U ∈ R^{d×d_ffn} expands channels. σ = GeLU. s(·) is the spatial interaction layer (SGU). V ∈ R^{d_ffn/2 × d} contracts back. Shortcuts/norms omitted for brevity.

equations.name: spatial_linear_projection
equations.description: Core spatial projection. W ∈ R^{n×n} operates on the sequence dimension. b ∈ R^{n×1} is token-specific bias. Unlike self-attention, W is input-independent (static).

symbols.W: [n, n] learnable spatial weight matrix (static, input-independent)
symbols.Z: [n, e] input (e = d_ffn/2 for the v branch)

equations.name: simple_multiplicative_gate
equations.description: Baseline multiplicative gating (no channel split). Full Z is both gate and gated signal.

equations.name: split_multiplicative_gate_SGU
equations.latex: s(Z) = Z_1 \odot f_{W,b}(Z_2), \quad Z = Z_1 \| Z_2
equations.description: Full SGU formula. Z is split channel-wise into two equal halves. Z1 acts as the multiplicative bypass gate; Z2 is spatially transformed and becomes the gating signal. This is the final SGU used in all reported results.
equations.role: spatial gating in all gMLP/aMLP experiments

equations.name: W_initialization
equations.description: Critical for training stability. W initialized near-zero so f_{W,b}(Z) ≈ 1 at start → s(Z) ≈ Z (identity). Each gMLP block starts as a regular FFN.
equations.note: ε not numerically specified in paper; 'near-zero' is the description. Common practice: truncated normal with std=0.02 or smaller.

equations.name: toeplitz_W
equations.description: When Toeplitz constraint is applied (MLM experiments), W is parameterized by a single vector w of size 2n-1. The matrix element W_ij depends only on the relative position i-j. This implements a full-sequence 1D convolution shared across channels.

equations.name: tiny_attention_score
equations.description: Standard scaled dot-product attention with a single head. d_attn ∈ {64, 128}. Q, K, V are projected from the pre-block normalized input.

tensors.name: X
tensors.role: input token representations to each block

tensors.name: Z2
tensors.role: SGU spatial branch (input to W projection)

tensors.name: W
tensors.role: spatial weight matrix — shared across channels of Z2

tensors.name: b
tensors.role: token-specific bias for spatial projection, init=1

tensors.name: w_toeplitz
tensors.role: Toeplitz parameterization vector for W (MLM experiments)

nlp_pretraining.note_masking: Standard BERT masking — not explicitly re-specified in this paper, assumed 15% uniform MLM

primary_results_table_vision.note: From Table 2 — ImageNet-1K without extra data

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Framework: PyTorch (not TF/JAX). Paper uses TF/TPU; we implement in PyTorch for GPU reproducibility.
W initialization: truncated normal with std=0.02 (standard Transformer init) but scaled by 0.1 to approximate near-zero. Bias init=1 for spatial projection.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says 'near-zero'; literature default is truncated_normal(std=0.02); scaling by ≤0.1 achieves near-zero behavior.
alternatives: zeros + noise during first few steps

implementation_assumptions.assumption: MLM masking: 15% of tokens masked, 80% [MASK] / 10% random / 10% original (standard BERT).
implementation_assumptions.basis: BERT protocol referenced; not re-specified in this paper.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Toeplitz matrix implementation in PyTorch via circulant trick or explicit parameterization via torch.Tensor of size 2n-1 with index masking.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper provides TF pseudocode (Appendix C); PyTorch equivalent uses similar padding+reshape.
alternatives: Explicit [n,n] matrix with weight-tying via scatter

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Vision stem: standard ViT patch embedding (Conv2d with kernel=16, stride=16 → flatten → linear projection to d_model).
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper states 'input and output protocols follow ViT/B16'.
alternatives: learned patch tokenizer with overlapping patches

implementation_assumptions.assumption: LayerNorm used throughout (both channel-wise in block and inside SGU on the Z2 branch before spatial proj). Pre-norm architecture.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Figure 1 pseudocode shows norm before every major op; consistent with modern BERT/ViT pre-norm style.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: aMLP combining mechanism: gate = W*Z2 + tiny_attn(X_pre); i.e., spatial proj output and tiny attention output are additively combined before multiplying with Z1.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Figure 6 shows addition symbol in diagram; pseudocode implies both contribute to gating signal.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Stochastic depth implementation: drop entire block residual with probability (1-survival_prob) during training, scaled at inference. Applied only in vision models.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard stochastic depth (Huang et al. 2016). Applied in vision; no mention of it in NLP.

ambiguities.description: aMLP: how exactly is tiny_attn combined with the SGU gating branch? Diagram shows '+' operator but text says 'attached to the gating function'. Whether the input to tiny_attn is the normalized pre-block input X or Z2 post-split is not definitively clear.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: tiny_attn takes the pre-block LayerNorm output (normalized X before channel expansion); its output is added to f_{W,b}(Z2) before the Z1 ⊙ gate multiply.
alternatives: tiny_attn output replaces f_{W,b}(Z2) rather than adding to it

ambiguities.description: Near-zero W initialization: exact std value not provided.
Whether CLS token is used in vision classification or global average pooling.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Global average pooling (consistent with ViT/B16 when no positional encoding is used; also simpler for no-positional-encoding models).
